lede: A look at May 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Cam Ward (American football) and A. J. Foyt IV, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: General Data Protection Regulation
events.desc: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) becomes enforceable in the European Union.

events.title: Coup d'état
events.desc: A military coup in Sierra Leone replaces President Ahmad Tejan Kabbah with Major Johnny Paul Koroma.

events.title: Killing of Joetha Collier
events.desc: Joetha Collier, a recent high school graduate, is killed in a shooting in Drew, Mississippi, attracting extensive attention from the media and civil rights activists.

events.title: Gateway Arch
events.desc: The Gateway Arch in St. Louis, Missouri, is dedicated.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German 2nd Panzer Division captures the port of Boulogne-sur-Mer; the surrender of the last French and British troops marks the end of the Battle of Boulogne.

events.title: The Walt Disney Company
events.desc: The Walt Disney Company cartoon Three Little Pigs premieres at Radio City Music Hall, featuring the hit song "Who's Afraid of the Big Bad Wolf?"

events.title: Oscar Wilde
events.desc: Playwright, poet and novelist Oscar Wilde is convicted of "committing acts of gross indecency with other male persons" and sentenced to serve two years in prison.

events.title: Gilbert and Sullivan
events.desc: Gilbert and Sullivan's comic opera H.M.S. Pinafore opens at the Opera Comique in London.

events.title: Irish Rebellion of 1798
events.desc: United Irishmen Rebellion: Battle of Carlow begins; executions of suspected rebels at Carnew and at Dunlavin Green take place.

events.title: Constitutional Convention (United States)
events.desc: After a delay of 11 days, the United States Constitutional Convention formally convenes in Philadelphia after a quorum of seven states is secured.

births.name: Jack Steinberger
births.line: German-Swiss physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate (died 2020)

deaths.text: George Floyd, African American man murdered by Minneapolis police officer Derek Chauvin
Renzo De Felice, Italian historian and author
Paula von Preradović, Croatian poet and author
